topic: Jetpack Compose vs XML layouts for Android

messages.content: Starting a new Android app. Jetpack Compose or stick with XML layouts? Our team knows XML.

**Jetpack Compose** for any new Android app in 2026:

- Declarative UI (like SwiftUI, React) — less code, easier reasoning
- No more `findViewById`, ViewBinding, or data binding
- Live previews in Android Studio
- Material 3 components built-in
- Animations are dramatically simpler
- Interop: Compose can embed XML views and vice versa

XML layouts are legacy. Google has shifted all documentation and new APIs to Compose. New features (like adaptive layouts for foldables) are Compose-only.

For your XML-experienced team: the mental model shift takes 1-2 weeks. Compose is much closer to React/SwiftUI than XML. Most developers prefer it once they get past the initial learning curve.

```kotlin
@Composable
fun UserCard(user: User) {
    Card(modifier = Modifier.padding(8.dp)) {
        Text(user.name, style = MaterialTheme.typography.headlineMedium)
        Text(user.email, style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyMedium)
    }
}
```
What about the navigation library? We use the old Fragment-based navigation.

Compose has its own navigation:

**Navigation Compose** (`androidx.navigation:navigation-compose`):
- Type-safe routes with Kotlin serialization (no more string-based routes)
- Deep linking support
- Shared element transitions
- Nested navigation graphs

```kotlin
@Serializable data class UserDetail(val userId: Int)

NavHost(navController, startDestination = Home) {
    composable<Home> { HomeScreen(onUserClick = { navController.navigate(UserDetail(it)) }) }
    composable<UserDetail> { backStackEntry ->
        val route = backStackEntry.toRoute<UserDetail>()
        UserDetailScreen(userId = route.userId)
    }
}
```

The type-safe navigation (added in Navigation 2.8+) is much better than the old string-based Fragment transactions. Compile-time checking of route parameters.

For a new project: Navigation Compose. For migration: you can mix Fragment navigation and Compose navigation during the transition.
